article_meta.title: Metro An open letter from the heart of Iran to America – Don’t give up on us By California Post Staff Published March 7, 2026, 7:11 p.m

reasoning: This article is an open letter/first-person account from an Iranian citizen ('Omid'), reproduced by the California Post/New York Post. The entire body of the article is essentially an extended reproduced statement from this source. Under the attribution rule, the language in that statement belongs to the source, not the author. So returning [] for the body text is largely correct. However, there are arguable author-level bias concerns: (1) The editorial decision to publish this letter without any counterbalancing perspective or context (Bias by Story Choice and Placement / Bias by Omission) - no alternative viewpoints on the bombing, no mention of civilian casualties from US strikes, no critical voices; (2) The headline framing 'from the heart of Iran' is the author/editor's own prose choice, using emotionally loaded language (Sensationalism/Emotionalism); (3) The article presents the letter writer's claims about civilian casualties from the regime ('more than 30,000 in just two days') without editorial verification or context. These are defensible author-level bias instances that were missed. The model correctly avoided flagging the source's quoted language, which is good. But it missed structural/framing bias choices made by the editors.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model correctly avoided flagging any of the source's extended statement as author bias. No attribution rule violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: The model returned an empty array, so there are zero false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed several defensible author-level bias instances: the headline's emotional framing ('from the heart of Iran'), the editorial choice to publish without any counterbalancing perspective (Bias by Omission/Story Choice), and the lack of any editorial context or verification of claims. However, because the article is largely a reproduced letter, many of these are debatable rather than clear-cut, so the miss is moderate rather than severe.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No bias types were applied since the output was empty, so there are no errors here.
